reasoning: RFK Jr. announced in early April 2025 that he plans to order the CDC to stop recommending fluoride, while HHS simultaneously convened a task force to study and issue new recommendations. Because federal task forces typically take many months to conclude, the task force is highly unlikely to issue a new recommendation before July 1, 2025. The main pathway for a 'NO' resolution is if the CDC pulls the webpage offline or strips the 0.7 mg/L recommendation pending the review. While the Trump administration has shown a willingness to abruptly pull health webpages, the fact that the page appears to not have been pulled down in the 6 weeks following the April announcement suggests the CDC may leave the guidance up while the task force conducts its review. Given the short timeframe until July 1 but the high political pressure from the HHS Secretary, I estimate a roughly even chance that the page is pulled or modified before the deadline.
